slides.title: Agent Teams
slides.subtitle: Teammates + Mailboxes
slides.motto: When one agent can't finish, delegate to persistent teammates via async mailboxes

slides.title: The Problem: Agent Teams
slides.problem:
Subagents (s04) are disposable: spawn, work, return summary, die. No identity, no memory between invocations. Background tasks (s08) run shell commands but can't make LLM-guided decisions.

Real teamwork needs: (1) persistent agents that outlive a single prompt, (2) identity and lifecycle management, (3) a communication channel between agents.

slides.title: The Solution: TeammateManager + file-based mailbox
slides.solution:
```
Teammate lifecycle:
  spawn -> WORKING -> IDLE -> WORKING -> ... -> SHUTDOWN

Communication:
  .team/
    config.json           <- team roster + statuses
    inbox/
      alice.jsonl         <- append-only, drain-on-read
      bob.jsonl
      lead.jsonl

              +--------+    send("alice","bob","...")    +--------+
              | alice  | -----------------------------> |  bob   |
              | loop   |    bob.jsonl << {json_line}    |  loop  |
              +--------+                                +--------+
                   ^                                         |
                   |        BUS.read_inbox("alice")          |
                   +---- alice.jsonl -> read + drain ---------+
```
principles: When one agent can't finish, delegate to persistent teammates via async mailboxes

slides.title: Key Takeaways
takeaways: When one agent can't finish, delegate to persistent teammates via async mailboxes
